articles.title: Codex, Cursor and the Great Beyond
articles.content:
早上好，

《AI霸权》也是一份通讯，我们追踪大型科技公司、大型人工智能公司与争夺未来市场份额的最佳人工智能初创公司之间的交集。

现有企业将面临压力。微软支持的OpenAI已经在某种程度上进入了现有企业领域。今天，让我们深入了解Codex。

今年3月，我们了解到OpenAI希望创建一个结合ChatGPT和Codex的"超级应用"。距离预计的IPO只有几个月时间，这对OpenAI来说是一个尴尬的转型，因为ChatGPT甚至已不再占据50%的市场份额。截至2026年1月，ChatGPT仍保持超过50%的市场份额，但到5月底已跌至46.4%，这主要受到Gemini（27.7%）和Claude（10.3%）崛起的冲击。随着Meta AI应用获得初步增长动力，到年底ChatGPT的市场地位将如何变化仍是未知数。同时，OpenAI向企业AI的转型也面临挑战——SpaceX刚刚完成了对Cursor的收购。

"过去几个月，SpaceXAI一直在与Cursor联合训练一个模型，将很快在Cursor和Grok Build中发布。"——SpaceX

与此同时，Meta AI应用正在经历巨大的增长势头，得益于其与Meta 35亿日活跃用户生态系统的深度整合、独立移动应用的增长，以及在整个产品线中推出"AI Mode"等新原生功能的激进推广。这能增长多快？

在企业AI领域，除Anthropic的主导地位外，Cursor的年经常性收入在6月达到40亿美元。因此OpenAI在B2C和B2B两个领域都面临竞争压力。这意味着OpenAI将ChatGPT与Codex合并的执行风险很高，结果可能决定这家企业在竞争激烈的市场中的未来。

他是《人工智能增强工程师》一书的作者。他将报道本文中关于Codex的部分。

Cursor加快收入增长并进入人工智能代理领域，使其成为帮助SpaceXAI扩大企业人工智能客户规模的理想选择，这些客户构成了SpaceX在其S-1文件中提及的可寻址市场的重要部分。虽然ChatGPT据说已达到10亿用户（月活跃用户），但其市场份额正在快速下滑。最新一期的2026年中期人工智能状态报告对此有详细阐述。

我非常喜欢SenseTower的报告，他们不是我们的关联公司。Sensor Tower的人工智能状态报告还发现，用户越来越愿意在不同的助手之间切换。ChatGPT将面临来自谷歌Gemini、Claude、SpaceXAI、Meta AI以及资金充足的DeepSeek、阿里巴巴通义千问、月之暗面和Z.AI以及字节跳动的压力，这些公司正在快速发展。Sense Tower的人工智能状态报告对密切关注人工智能的人来说还有很多细节。比如，Anthropic的Claude每用户收入比ChatGPT更高。在企业业务和对编码模型的专注优势方面，这实际上是一个更好的商业策略。Claude在美国的移动端每用户平均收入从去年9月的不到0.50美元飙升至今年5月的2.76美元。今天的文章将包含许多与OpenAI Codex相关的有趣链接。

阅读：Codex希望超越代码工作

使用NotebookLM学习新知识

Codex目标的终极指南

OpenAI如何提示Codex以获得最佳结果

与此同时，同时代的公司和更年轻的人工智能初创企业正在获得大量资金，这将挑战OpenAI的主导地位。我预计中国的人工智能生态系统将在2027年实现突破性发展。

SpaceX预计合并将在今年第三季度完成，他们已经在新编码模型上快速合作。这给了曾经相对失败且烧钱严重的xAI在收入和动力方面的重大提升。由于SpaceX IPO的巨大成功，这也完全改变了格局，给OpenAI带来了更大压力。在撰写本文时，股价自上周五IPO以来已上涨31%，市值超过了亚马逊，甚至一度短暂超越了微软的市值。

我已经相信Anthropic和谷歌正在对OpenAI施加压力，并在某些方面已经超越了它。现在SpaceXAI和Meta AI通过其人工智能应用的崛起意味着OpenAI很可能会继续面临困境。

这一切完全改变了人工智能的未来。

Cursor以40亿美元的运行率退出，同比增长7倍。这样的MIT初创企业成功故事在技术史上很难找到。四位25岁的MIT创始人将在4年内各获得约27亿美元，而最初的50位初创企业员工每人将获得2000万至5亿美元。这是有史以来最大的人工智能编码退出案例。

他们本可以在SpaceX敲门前获得另一轮融资，当时Cursor预计将从Andreessen Horowitz、Thrive和Nvidia等公司获得20亿美元的融资轮，使这家人工智能编码初创企业的估值达到500亿美元。但这从未实现，从与SpaceX的计算合作交易迅速升级为收购交易。由于计算成本高昂，Cursor尽管增长迅速但烧钱太快。SpaceX负债累累且无利可图，却能吸收这类亏损。

假设你对使用Codex作为人工智能工具持开放态度。

OpenAI Academy（不是我们的关联公司）有很多关于使用Codex的研讨会和现场会议。过去几年，OpenAI在营销和教育方面投入了大量资金，但Codex可能会演变成一个有用的通用工具。如果OpenAI希望有盈利之路，Codex需要表现出色。

生成式人工智能趋势是一个关于快速变化的商业和消费者偏好以及越来越多的选择的故事。

我在LinkedIn和Substack Notes上进行了类似的投票，似乎正在形成一种共识。在上述投票中，我加入了SpaceX，因为Cursor的收购大大改变了xAI糟糕的执行和表现。

OpenAI的Codex计划故事更加特殊。现在让我来介绍。

Codex过去很容易解释。

它是OpenAI为开发者提供的工具。你把它指向一个代码库，给它一个编码任务，它帮助你把想法变成可用的软件。如果你听说过Claude Code，它就是OpenAI版本的这个工具。

但正在发生一些奇怪的事情。Codex开始超越代码。

OpenAI正把Codex从一个开发者工具转变为一个用于知识工作的通用代理。Gmail、日历、PowerPoint、计算机使用，甚至即将与ChatGPT合并。

Codex不再只是软件工程师自动化编码任务的地方。它正在成为OpenAI希望工作发生的地方。

阅读更多
articles.summary:
Good Morning,

AI Supremacy is also a Newsletter where we track the intersection between BigTech, BigAI and the best AI startups competing for future marketshare.

  • Incumbents will be put under pressure. Microsoft backed OpenAI is sort of in incumbent territory already. Today, let’s take a closer look into Codex.

  • In March we learned that OpenAI want to create a “Super-app” that combines ChatGPT and Codex. With just a few months before a supposed IPO, that’s an awkward pivot for OpenAI, where ChatGPT no longer even has a 50% marketshare.

Until January, 2026 ChatGPT commanded over 50% market share, but by May's end, it had fallen to 46.4% thanks to the rise of Gemini (27.7%) and Claude (10.3%). Now with Meta’s AI app gaining initial momentum, who knows where ChatGPT will even be by year’s end. There’s also a problem with OpenAI’s pivot to Enterprise AI, SpaceX just finished the acquisition of Cursor.

“For the past few months, SpaceXAI has been jointly training a model with Cursor, which will be released in Cursor and Grok Build soon.” - SpaceX

Why OpenAI is Under Pressure in Marketshare

Meanwhile the Meta AI app is experiencing a massive surge in momentum, driven by deep integration into Meta's 3.5 billion daily active users ecosystem, standalone mobile app growth, and aggressive rollouts of new native features like "AI Mode" across Facebook. How fast can that increase?

On the Enterprise AI side outside of Anthropic’s dominance, Cursor’s ARR hit $4 Billion in June. OpenAI thus has competitive pressures both in B2C and B2B. This means OpenAI’s execution risk in merging ChatGPT with Codex is high and the result might determine what kind of future the business will have in such a competitive market.

is the author of The AI-Augmented Engineer. He will be covering the Codex section of the article.

The AI-Augmented Engineer
Teaching software engineers how to accelerate their careers with AI. Deep, technical guides on Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and more.
By Jeff Morhous

Cursor’s acceleration of revenue and entry into AI agents makes it an ideal fit to help scale SpaceXAI’s bold ambitions to scale Enterprise AI customers that makes up a generous part of SpaceX’s speculative TAM (total addressable market in its S-1) to say the least. While ChatGPT has hit a supposed 1 Billion users (MAUs), it’s losing marketshare very rapidly. The latest iteration of a State of AI mid 2026 report goes into some great details:

I really enjoyed this SenseTower report, they are not an affiliate.

View the Report

ChatGPT’s 3.5 Year First Mover Advantage is Shrinking

Sensor Tower’s State of AI Report also found that users are increasingly willing to switch between assistants. ChatGPT will be put under pressure by Google’s Gemini, Claude, SpaceXAI, Meta AI as well as a well funded DeepSeek, Alibaba Qwen, Moonshot AI and Z.AI and ByteDance that are evolving rapidly.

Sense Towers State of AI Report has a lot of tidbits as well for those of us who follow AI closely. Like for instance, that Anthropic’s Claude earns more revenue per user than ChatGPT does. That emphasis on B2B and superiority in coding models is really turning out to be a better commercial strategy.

ChatGPT fast to 1 Billion MAUs but Trouble is Ahead

Claude’s average revenue per user on mobile in the US rocketed from under $0.50 last September to $2.76 in May.

Today’s article will have many interesting OpenAI Codex related links.

  • Read: Codex wants to work on more than just code

  • Using NotebookLM to learn new things

  • The ultimate guide to Codex goals

  • How OpenAI prompts Codex for the best results

Meanwhile both contemporaries and younger AI startups are getting serious funding that will challenge OpenAI’s dominance. I project China’s AI ecosystem to have a really breakout 2027.

SpaceXAI Acquiring Cursor (Anysphere) for $60 Billion

SpaceX expects the merger to close during the third quarter of this year and they are already collaborating rapidly on new coding models. This gives what was formerly xAI (a relative failure vs. cash burn), a serious boost in revenue and momentum. It also entirely changes the landscape and puts more pressure on OpenAI, given the wild success of the SpaceX IPO. At time the time of writing ticker is up 31% since its IPO last Friday with a market cap that overtook Amazon and even briefly reaching the market cap of Microsoft.

I had already believed Anthropic and Google were putting pressure on OpenAI and had overtaken them in certain ways. Now the rise of SpaceXAI and Meta AI via their AI app means OpenAI is highly likely to continue to struggle.

All of this totally changes the future of AI.

Cursor exits with a $4B in run rate growing 7x YoY. It’s hard to find such an MIT startup success story in the history of technology. The four 25yo MIT founders will make ~$2.7B (each) in a span 4yrs and first 50 startup hires ~$20-500M each. It’s an AI coding exit for the ages.

They could have gotten another round in funding where before SpaceX came knocking, Cursor was on track to close a $2 billion funding round from the likes of Andreessen Horowitz, Thrive, and Nvidia that would have valued the AI coding startup at $50 billion. But that never materialized, getting a compute deal with SpaceX quickly escalated into an acquisition deal. With the costs of compute, Cursor was burning cash too fast (at that point in time) even with its meteoric growth. SpaceX being heavily in debt and unprofitable, can absorb those types of loses.

Where to learn more about working with Codex

Let’s say you have an open mind to using Codex as an AI tool.

The OpenAI Academy (not an affiliate) has a lot of workshops and live sessions on using Codex. OpenAI’s spending on marketing and education has been considerable the past few years, but then again Codex might evolve into a useful general purpose tool. If OpenAI ever hopes to have a path to profitability, Codex needs to shine.

  • ⚡ Codex for everyday use

  • 🖥️ Building websites with Codex Sites

  • 💐 Codex for creative building

ChatGPT’s marketshare is declining rapidly

The Generative AI trend is a story of rapidly shifting business and consumer preferences and more and more options.

I am Obsessed with AI and Trust - Public Poll:

I conducted similar polls on LinkedIn and Substack Notes and there appears to be a growing consensus. In the above poll I add SpaceX as the Cursor acquisition changes xAI’s abysmal execution and performance substantially.

The story of OpenAI’s Codex plan is even more particular. I’ll turn it over to now.

What’s new in OpenAI’s Codex

Codex used to be easy to explain.

It was OpenAI’s tool for developers. You pointed it at a repo, gave it a coding task, and it helped you turn ideas into working software. If you’ve heard of Claude Code, it was just OpenAI’s version of that tool.

But something weird is happening. Codex is starting to outgrow code.

OpenAI is turning Codex from a developer tool into a general-purpose agent for knowledge work. Gmail, Calendar, PowerPoint, computer use, and even an upcoming merge with ChatGPT.

Codex is no longer just where software engineers go to automate coding tasks. It’s becoming the place where OpenAI wants work to happen.

Codex has historically been for developer

Read more
